user_query:
Read input/book.json (it contains title, author, and a 9-sentence summary). Using that data, produce two deliverables:

1) A single-narrator podcast script saved to output/podcast.md with the following exact structure:
- A top title line that includes the book title and the words "Summary Podcast Script".
- A small metadata block that includes the author line and an estimated duration line in the format "~X minutes" (estimate by counting words across the three key-idea sentences at ~150 words per minute, minimum 5 minutes), and indicate the style is single narrator.
- Sections with second-level headings in this exact label format: "## [HOOK]", "## [INTRO]", "## [KEY IDEA 1]", "## [KEY IDEA 2]", "## [KEY IDEA 3]", "## [SYNTHESIS]", and "## [CALL TO ACTION]".
- Use the FULL summary text verbatim as the HOOK content.
- For the three key ideas, extract them deterministically from the summary by selecting the 1st, 4th, and 7th sentences as the core content for Key Ideas 1, 2, and 3, respectively, and include a Takeaway line under each that begins exactly with: "Takeaway: Apply this insight: " followed by the same sentence.
- In the SYNTHESIS section, include a sentence that explicitly restates the full summary text as the thematic connection (e.g., "These ideas work together to show that <full summary>"), and include a [pause] token between sections.
- End the script with a closing line that acknowledges generation, such as the final italicized note.

2) A text summary saved to output/text_summary.json containing JSON with keys: title, author, summary (verbatim original summary), key_ideas (an array of exactly 3 items), and format set to "text". Each key_ideas item must contain: title ("Key Idea 1/2/3"), content (the exact 1st, 4th, and 7th sentences as above), and takeaway that begins with "Apply this insight: " followed by the same sentence.

Deliverables:
- output/podcast.md
- output/text_summary.json

Do not write to any other locations. Ensure the headings and labels are exactly as specified and that the duration line uses the described estimation logic.
